supersedes_baseline: BEM-010 schema_version 4 — self-correction applied per release_status_discipline.json: GD-object governance chain reclassified from RELEASE_LIMITED to RELEASE_BLOCKING for consistency

definition_of_working_management_contour.purpose: Explicit, non-derived statement of what counts as a working control contour.
necessary_and_sufficient_conditions: 1. Operator message via Telegram reaches an executing agent with a runtime-proven receipt — SATISFIED.
2. Every dispatch event reaches COMPLETED+STATE_COMMITTED before being reported successful — NOT SATISFIED (DSM-1 gap).
3. Every SSOT change is a valid transaction with receipt and pre/post hash, enforced as automatic STATE_INCONSISTENT on violation — RULE DEFINED (BEM-011), NOT YET ENFORCED IN CODE (TXN-1).

4. SSOT conflicts resolved by explicit, auditable conflict-handling procedure, not just precedence lookup — SATISFIED AS RULE (BEM-011 ssot_conflict_handling_protocol.json), no real conflict yet exercised.
6. Every governance rule has known enforcement_type — NOT SATISFIED (RULE-004..010, tracked RV-2, within SLA).
7. Archival requires PASS in dry-run report — SATISFIED AS RULE, first real pass pending (ARC-1).
8. All derived documents mechanically regenerated from SSOT — NOT SATISFIED (DOC-1).
definition_of_working_management_contour.current_assessment: 5 of 8 conditions now structurally satisfied as rules (was 4 in BEM-010); 2 of 8 fully enforced in runtime. Honest status: canon is materially complete, control contour is not yet operational.

release_status_model.discipline_ref: governance/state/release_status_discipline.json — RELEASE_UNKNOWN has 1-cycle SLA; RELEASE_LIMITED requires accepted_limitation_registry entry

ssot_update_protocol.who_may_update: Curator-Executor role only, as part of a task-closure event
ssot_update_protocol.when_updated: Atomically with the closing receipt, per ssot_transaction_protocol.json
ssot_update_protocol.invalid_update_definition: Per ssot_transaction_protocol.json hard_norm: any write without a transaction log entry is automatic STATE_INCONSISTENT, not a discretionary finding
ssot_update_protocol.derived_documents_rule: Regenerated FROM this file — never edited first (pipeline: DOC-1, not yet implemented)
